title: Sampling, randomization, and blinding: what a study's number is about

objectives.statement: Explain what a study's eligibility and recruitment route establishes about the population its estimate describes, and why a route applied before allocation bounds external validity without damaging the comparison between arms.

Explain how random allocation makes compared groups comparable in expectation on measured and unmeasured factors, distinguish it from predictable schemes such as alternation or record number, and state what it does not guarantee in a single study.
Distinguish allocation concealment from blinding by the moment each acts and the error each prevents, and identify which safeguard a described procedure broke.
Audit an unfamiliar described study across entry, allocation, foreknowledge, and awareness, then state the strongest warranted inference and one material boundary on it.

scenes.title: What a study's number is actually about
Auditing a study in time order
Four questions, less and less help
Taking the four questions somewhere else
